title: SonicWall Content Filtering Service Blocks by Category (SonicWall)
dataSources: `sourcetype=dell:sonicwall:firewall` CFS category in `cat` or URL category field
spl:
index=netfw sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall" earliest=-7d
| where match(lower(fw_action),"(?i)block|deny") AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)cfs|category|web")
| stats count by cat, usr, src_ip
| sort -count
| head 50
description: CFS blocks are primary evidence of acceptable-use enforcement. Category trending reveals shadow IT, compromised clients, or policy drift.
value: Improves HR/legal alignment on web policy and speeds investigations into malware C2 categories.
implementation: Map vendor category integers to human-readable names via lookup `sonicwall_cfs_categories.csv`.
visualization: Stacked bar (categories over time), table (user, category, URL host).

references.title: Splunkbase — Dell SonicWall Add-on for Splunk
Splunk Connect for Syslog — Dell SonicWall reference

grandmaExplanation: CFS blocks are primary evidence of acceptable-use enforcement — we monitor SonicWall logs so teams can act before users feel pain.
